jokes.text: Wow, dude! I thought my place looked like shit.
jokes.setup: Dennis enters Charlie's apartment
jokes.punchline: His own place is apparently terrible, but Charlie's is somehow worse
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical line that establishes both characters' living situations while being genuinely funny. Good character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Wow, dude! I thought my place looked like shit.

jokes.text: No. Yeah, it's... I found out I might have cancer, so ooh.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks if Charlie looks hungover
jokes.punchline: Casual, dismissive delivery of fake cancer news with 'so ooh' ending
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect Charlie delivery - casual about life-threatening illness. The 'so ooh' is perfectly awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No. Yeah, it's... I found out I might have cancer, so ooh.

jokes.text: Uh, did you want to talk, like, right now?
jokes.setup: Charlie wants to discuss his 'cancer diagnosis'
jokes.punchline: Dennis's immediate discomfort at having to be supportive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - selfish even when friend reveals cancer. The awkward phrasing sells his discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Uh, did you want to talk, like, right now?

jokes.text: It's just that Mac and Sweet Dee are down in the car waitin' for me. I told them I'd just, you know, zip in and zip out.
jokes.setup: Charlie wants emotional support for cancer
jokes.punchline: Dennis prioritizes basketball plans over friend's terminal illness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Dennis is so self-centered he can't even fake compassion properly.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's just that Mac and Sweet Dee are down in the car waitin' for me. I told them I'd just, you know, zip in and zip out.

jokes.text: Could I get the... the basketball though? It's just we're supposed to meet, like, a bunch of people... and we were supposed to bring that.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment after cancer revelation
jokes.punchline: Dennis still prioritizes getting the basketball over his friend's crisis
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Dennis can't help himself. The hesitant delivery makes it even more awful and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Could I get the... the basketball though? It's just we're supposed to meet, like, a bunch of people... and we were supposed to bring that.

jokes.text: And we cried together, and I held him for a while... and that was it.
jokes.setup: Mac asks what happened with Charlie
jokes.punchline: Dennis lies about showing compassion he clearly didn't show
jokes.explanation: Dennis rewriting history to make himself look good. Works because audience saw what really happened.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And we cried together, and I held him for a while... and that was it.

jokes.text: That is so sad. He is gonna look so bad without that hair.
jokes.setup: Learning about Charlie's cancer
jokes.punchline: Dee's first concern is Charlie's appearance, not his health
jokes.explanation: Pure Dee - shallow and self-centered. Prioritizing aesthetics over mortality is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That is so sad. He is gonna look so bad without that hair.

jokes.text: What's the one thing that Charlie never gets? - Laid.
jokes.setup: Mac asks what Charlie never gets
jokes.punchline: Dennis's immediate, blunt answer
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline with perfect economy. Dennis's matter-of-fact delivery sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What's the one thing that Charlie never gets?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Laid.

jokes.text: So Charlie's wasting away, and we're gonna talk about Sweet Dee's feelings all morning.
jokes.setup: Dee refuses to help with their plan
jokes.punchline: Mac sarcastically prioritizes Charlie's fake cancer over real work
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery, though 'wasting away' is a bit obvious. Works for character dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So Charlie's wasting away, and we're gonna talk about Sweet Dee's feelings all morning.

jokes.text: Dennis, our bar is in South Philly in a scary alley. Might as well call it Rape Bar.
jokes.setup: Explaining why there are no women in their bar
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment with shocking alternative name
jokes.explanation: Dark, shocking, and memorable. 'Rape Bar' is brutally funny and perfectly captures the show's willingness to go dark.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Dennis, our bar is in South Philly in a scary alley. Might as well call it Rape Bar.

jokes.text: Carmen, you have the most amazing body. Are you bulimic, or what?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to flirt with Carmen
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate question delivered as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - thinks he's smooth while being completely inappropriate. The casual delivery makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Carmen, you have the most amazing body. Are you bulimic, or what?

jokes.text: That's a dude. - No. The one with the exposed midriff and the cans.
jokes.setup: Mac pointing out a girl for Charlie
jokes.punchline: Mac defending his choice despite Dee's correction
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about Carmen being transgender. Mac's obliviousness is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That's a dude.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No. The one with the exposed midriff and the cans.

jokes.text: There's an unmistakable bulge of a large penis in those jeans. - There's a dick in those pants.
jokes.setup: Examining whether Carmen is male or female
jokes.punchline: Mac's clinical description followed by Dennis's blunt confirmation
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective escalation. The contrast between Mac's formal language and Dennis's bluntness works.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: There's an unmistakable bulge of a large penis in those jeans.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There's a dick in those pants.

jokes.text: Is that a penis in your pants? - Yeah. - You lied to me.
jokes.setup: Dennis confronting Carmen about gender
jokes.punchline: Dennis thinks Carmen lied about working out, not about gender
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - audience expects Dennis to be upset about the penis, but he's mad about the workout lie. Perfect Dennis narcissism.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Is that a penis in your pants?

dialogue.speaker: Carmen
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You lied to me.

jokes.text: What time are... are you getting off of work? - It's not a thing to walk away about. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Charlie hitting on the waitress wearing a Lance Armstrong bracelet
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confused reaction to her walking away
jokes.explanation: Charlie's social awkwardness in full display. The confused 'whatever' is perfectly pathetic.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What time are... are you getting off of work?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's not a thing to walk away about. Whatever.

jokes.text: You know what that symbolizes? Those bracelets symbolize jumping on fashion trends. And you know what the thing that's so rad is? They come in all kinds of colors now, so you can accessorize your compassion with your outfits.
jokes.setup: Mac saying the bracelet shows the waitress is compassionate
jokes.punchline: Dee's cynical take on charity bracelet fashion trends
jokes.explanation: 'Accessorize your compassion' is a great line. Dee being cynical about performative charity is spot-on.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You know what that symbolizes? Those bracelets symbolize jumping on fashion trends. And you know what the thing that's so rad is? They come in all kinds of colors now, so you can accessorize your compassion with your outfits.

jokes.text: She is in... way over her head. - In what? - Debt.
jokes.setup: Mac cryptically saying Dee is 'in'
jokes.punchline: The dramatic pause followed by mundane 'debt'
jokes.explanation: Good use of dramatic pause for comedic effect. The anticlimax works well.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She is in... way over her head.
In what?
Debt.

jokes.text: How about that game against Dallas... when Santa came out at halftime and we threw batteries?
jokes.setup: Mac trying to explain Dennis grabbing Dee
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to throwing batteries at Santa
jokes.explanation: Dark humor referencing Philadelphia's notorious reputation. Casual delivery of horrible behavior is perfectly Sunny.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: How about that game against Dallas... when Santa came out at halftime and we threw batteries?

jokes.text: Charlie has cancer.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking if they really need to let Dee punch him
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that justify anything. Mac's deadpan delivery sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie has cancer.

jokes.text: I'm just a small-town girl... who moved to the big city with big dreams... just to find out... the only way to make it in the big city... is to shake it. And that's what I do... at the Coyote Ugly.
jokes.setup: Artemis doing an acting scene
jokes.punchline: Overwrought monologue ending with Coyote Ugly reference
jokes.explanation: Establishes Artemis as pretentious and dramatic. The Coyote Ugly reference dates it but fits the character.

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: I'm just a small-town girl... who moved to the big city with big dreams... just to find out... the only way to make it in the big city... is to shake it. And that's what I do... at the Coyote Ugly.

jokes.text: I won't be able to pay my bills! I'm thinkin' about goin' on welfare! What am I gonna do?
jokes.setup: Artemis saying she lost her job at Wawa
jokes.punchline: Dramatic breakdown over losing a convenience store job
jokes.explanation: Artemis treating minimum wage job loss like Shakespearean tragedy. The escalation works for the character.

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: I won't be able to pay my bills! I'm thinkin' about goin' on welfare! What am I gonna do?

jokes.text: Uh, Rick Astley. What's up?
jokes.setup: Dee asks what Dennis is listening to
jokes.punchline: Dennis casually admitting to listening to Rick Astley
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual admission undermines his cool guy image. Simple but effective character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Uh, Rick Astley. What's up?

jokes.text: She's gettin' it removed. - So what? - I'm just puttin' in my time, you know? So that afterwards I'll be... - Gross, dude. - First.
jokes.setup: Dennis dating Carmen who's getting gender reassignment surgery
jokes.punchline: Dennis waiting to be 'first' after surgery
jokes.explanation: Incredibly dark and wrong, but perfectly Dennis. Mac's 'gross' reaction makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: She's gettin' it removed.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So what?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm just puttin' in my time, you know? So that afterwards I'll be...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Gross, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: First.

jokes.text: Charlie has cancer.
jokes.setup: Dennis approaching the waitress
jokes.punchline: Blunt delivery of Charlie's fake diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Callback to Mac's line, but Dennis uses it as a pickup strategy. Economy is perfect.

dialogue.line: Charlie has cancer.

jokes.text: You did what? - I slept with her.
jokes.setup: Mac expecting Dennis helped Charlie with the waitress
jokes.punchline: Dennis slept with her himself instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis move - uses friend's fake cancer for personal gain. Mac's shock sells the betrayal.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You did what?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I slept with her.

jokes.text: Cancer... gold mine.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining how mentioning cancer got him sex
jokes.punchline: Treating cancer as a pickup strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor. Two words that perfectly capture Dennis's sociopathy. Extremely quotable and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Cancer... gold mine.

jokes.text: Gross. - 250. - Deal.
jokes.setup: Mac offering Carmen $200 to sleep with Charlie
jokes.punchline: Carmen's instant acceptance when price increases
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy showing everyone has a price. The quick turnaround from disgust to acceptance is great.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Gross.

dialogue.speaker: Carmen
dialogue.line: Deal.

jokes.text: Charlie has cancer.
jokes.setup: Mac telling Carmen about Charlie
jokes.punchline: Third time this line is used, now as manipulation tactic
jokes.explanation: Running gag loses some impact through repetition, but works as manipulation tool.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie has cancer.

jokes.text: I'm Artemis.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking who she is
jokes.punchline: Artemis's dramatic self-introduction
jokes.explanation: Artemis treating her name announcement like a major reveal. Establishes her theatrical personality.

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: I'm Artemis.

jokes.text: I shake it. Shake it. I shake it. I shake it. I shake it. I sha...
jokes.setup: Artemis doing her 'audition' dance
jokes.punchline: Awkward repetitive dancing that goes on too long
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy through awkwardness, but goes on too long without escalation. Poor economy.

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: I shake it. Shake it. I shake it. I shake it. I shake it. I sha...

jokes.text: I feel like an ice cream sundae, you know?
jokes.setup: Charlie describing how good he feels
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to ice cream sundae
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie weirdness - completely absurd comparison that somehow makes emotional sense for him.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I feel like an ice cream sundae, you know?

jokes.text: Here's the thing though... about cancer. I don't have it.
jokes.setup: Charlie seeming happy about his 'cancer' situation
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that he never had cancer
jokes.explanation: Major plot reveal delivered with perfect Charlie casualness. The pause before 'I don't have it' is perfectly timed.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Here's the thing though... about cancer. I don't have it.

jokes.text: Oh, no. You know what? It's a dude. Yeah, she has a penis. So it's okay.
jokes.setup: Mac pointing out domestic violence
jokes.punchline: Dennis saying it's okay because Carmen has a penis
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about domestic violence and transphobia. Dennis's casual bigotry is appalling but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, no. You know what? It's a dude. Yeah, she has a penis. So it's okay.

jokes.text: Sometimes you gotta crack a few eggs to make an omelet.
jokes.setup: Charlie justifying his cancer lie
jokes.punchline: Using cooking metaphor for emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Charlie misusing common wisdom. The cliché works because Charlie thinks he's being profound.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Sometimes you gotta crack a few eggs to make an omelet.

jokes.text: The waitress doesn't even like you. We had to pay her $250 to have sex with you!
jokes.setup: Charlie getting increasingly defensive
jokes.punchline: Dennis revealing they paid for sex, crushing Charlie's delusion
jokes.explanation: Brutal revelation that destroys Charlie's happiness. Dennis's cruelty is shocking but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The waitress doesn't even like you. We had to pay her $250 to have sex with you!

jokes.text: We didn't have sex.
jokes.setup: Everyone assuming Charlie had sex with the waitress
jokes.punchline: Charlie revealing nothing actually happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - even when paying for sex, he still can't close the deal. The economy makes it hit harder.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We didn't have sex.

jokes.text: I accidentally punched that tranny in the face. These two guys started chasing me.
jokes.setup: Mac bursting in excited to tell a story
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of assault and subsequent chase
jokes.explanation: Dark humor but relies on outdated terminology. Mac's obliviousness to the severity works for character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I accidentally punched that tranny in the face. These two guys started chasing me.

jokes.text: Uh, we paid you $250 to sleep with Charlie and... And you never actually had sex with him. So we were wondering if we could get the 250 bucks back.
jokes.setup: Confronting the waitress at her job
jokes.punchline: Requesting refund for prostitution that didn't result in sex
jokes.explanation: Dennis treating prostitution like a business transaction. The awkward politeness makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Uh, we paid you $250 to sleep with Charlie and... And you never actually had sex with him. So we were wondering if we could get the 250 bucks back.

jokes.text: Uh, we paid you $250 to sleep with Charlie and... And you never actually had sex with him. So we were wondering if we could get the 250 bucks back.
jokes.setup: Confronting waitress about the payment
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asking for refund on unused prostitution
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - treating prostitution like a business transaction with customer service. Peak gang awfulness.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Uh, we paid you $250 to sleep with Charlie and... And you never actually had sex with him. So we were wondering if we could get the 250 bucks back.

jokes.text: She's probably gettin' the money though, right?
jokes.setup: Waitress has locked the door
jokes.punchline: Mac's optimistic misreading of the situation
jokes.explanation: Mac's eternal optimism in the face of obvious rejection. Good ending beat.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She's probably gettin' the money though, right?
